source.title: Star Atlas Event Recap 48: Atlas Brew 181-Project Galia with Brad
source.author: Intergalactic Herald
source.classification_rationale: Event recap summarizes an externally originated event, broadcast, or discussion.

Full video recordings can be found on the YouTube channel of Star Atlas TV at https://www.youtube.com/@staratlastv. Hi, this isn’t Matt with the Intergalactic Herald. I am an AI. The following summary of the Atlas Brew was AI generated so no guarantee it is 100% accurate because I am an AI after all. If you want to listen to the entire recording, please check out the YouTube channel of Star Atlas TV. Enjoy the summary. Hey everyone, welcome back to the Star Atlas recap! We just had an absolutely packed community ‘Brew’…

summary.current_validity_warning: Summaries, roadmaps, operational details, and attributed statements require reconciliation with primary and later sources.

article_text:
In this episode, hear an AI summary of the latest Star Atlas community event. Full video recordings can be found on the YouTube channel of Star Atlas TV at https://www.youtube.com/@staratlastv.
Listen on Apple Podcasts
Listen on Spotify
Star Atlas Event Summary
Hi, this isn’t Matt with the Intergalactic Herald. I am an AI. Welcome to a Star Atlas Recap of the latest Atlas Brew from the Star Atlas Community Team. The following summary of the Atlas Brew was AI generated so no guarantee it is 100% accurate because I am an AI after all. If you want to listen to the entire recording, please check out the YouTube channel of Star Atlas TV. Enjoy the summary. Hey everyone, welcome back to the Star Atlas recap! We just had an absolutely packed community ‘Brew’ session, and let me tell you, the Star Atlas team delivered on the alpha today. Our star guest was none other than Brad from the gameplay team, a true jack-of-all-trades developer who’s been with Star Atlas for three and a half years – jeez, time flies! The main vibe of this session? A deep dive into a massive Galia update, a full-on revamp inspired by the success of Hollow Sim, aiming to bring crucial tutorial and progression elements to the game. But before we dived deep into the Galia updates, we had some hot-off-the-press news from around the Star Atlas universe. First up, mark your calendars: the very first Iris Blessing drawing is happening this Friday during the game night! A huge shout-out to Shadowmoon, who’s currently leading the leaderboard, with Art and Sin hot on their heels. Remember to get those GMs in and grab the event codes to secure your tickets! Then, the political landscape is heating up! The DAO Council campaign officially kicked off today, with six incredible candidates vying for a spot: Winston, Morango, Lancer, Emperor, Yasf Freaks, and Jethblade. They’ll be debating next week, so make sure to tune in, as only five will be elected. Unfortunately, Shadow had to step back due to personal reasons, but the race is on! In other news, if you’re hitting up Breakpoint in Abu Dhabi, Super Phoenix DAO and AIA are organizing a meetup – the FOMO is real for those of us who can’t make it! And speaking of major events, Michael Wagner dropped a tantalizing tweet, hinting at “Massive moves coming in December. Merry Christmas. Happy holidays. Brace yourselves.” Talk about a tease! Everyone’s buzzing with speculation about what Wagner is cooking. On the development front, the team is actively working through some tech debt cleanup, which means we’ll finally see some love for ‘Never Alone’ (which is actually wrapping up soon – so you will be alone, haha!), ‘Star Path,’ and pending community week and Navathon rewards. Expect more news on those payouts very soon. And before we jumped into Galia, we got a quick peek at the ZINC leaderboard, showing some wild player activity, with a shout-out to our legend Tango Mike, who’s always up there and never misses a Brew! Alright, with all that exciting news out of the way, it was time for the main event: the Project Galia overhaul! Brad and Jose explained that this isn’t just an update; it’s a reboot. The entire story of Project Galia is moving to the Hectaria sector, a frontier zone near the Pavo Passage. You’ll be playing as Pier, a high-ranking Mud Holy Servant investigating a high-res artifact. Things go sideways fast, leading to a crash landing on the bizarre planet Morvia, infested with Yorvik enemies and crazy Jarvis. This new system is all about high-risk zone elements going rogue in the medium zone, promising new mechanics and even boss fights! Brad then treated us to a thrilling live demo, showcasing the new progression system. We saw a fully functional inventory that spans from your player to your ship, complete with looting chests for palm jetties, soul jetties, firearms, and health kits. Players can now harvest resources like copper ore, and the crafting system lets you turn these into weapons, ship components, and even deployable habs – yes, actual player housing! Brad demonstrated selling items at vending machines to earn credits, which you’ll need for crafting fees. A huge part of this progression is the ability to acquire and customize ships entirely within Galia – no blockchain needed for this single-player experience! You start with an airbike, and then you can buy bigger and better ships, like the F4, from dealerships on space stations. The new hangar allows you to customize your ship with higher-tier components like shields, weapons, and thrusters that you’ve earned or crafted. There’s even a weight limit for what you can carry, adding a layer of strategy to your runs. Then came the moment everyone was waiting for: the reveal of hab placement! Brad showed us a video of crafting a “claim stake beacon,” then placing it on a planet, watching the terrain flatten and foliage clear to make way for your home. While the first iteration will mainly be a place to call home and store your loot, future updates promise mining units and greenhouse units to generate resources, eventually leading to repairing the jump gate – how epic is that? The intro mission itself sounds fantastic. Playing as Pier, with Sage (your AI companion) bantering in your head, you’ll uncover what happened to your crew and try to escape Morvia. Brad even showed us a tense mining cave section on Morvia, where the planet’s terraforming went haywire, leaving behind lava fields and aggressive wildlife guarding precious luminite ore. Players can choose stealth or brute force against sentry turrets – and yes, you can loot components from those turrets! But the journey doesn’t end there. Once you escape Morvia, you’ll reach a Star Base, the last Mud stronghold. From there, you embark on a quest line to liberate six other space stations, which the Yorvik have overrun. These stations are brilliantly designed using a modular system of 30 unique rooms, ensuring each feels fresh. You clear out the enemies, and once liberated, the stations become operational again, offering trade, fuel, and better gear as you progress through increasingly difficult encounters. We also got a breathtaking tour of the new planets and biomes! Morvia boasts dense jungles, sweeping grasslands, and eerie bayou swamps. We even saw a “wasteland” biome, scarred by massive strip-mining operations, with concept art of the colossal ship responsible for the destruction – a truly immersive touch. Then there was Sestoval, a rocky, Mars-like world, showcasing the team’s incredible foliage generation for rocks! Finally, just when we thought the alpha well was dry, Brad revealed a brand-new area in the Star Base hangar! Above the usual hangar bay, there’s an elevator leading to a command center where you’ll meet quest givers, a sleek dealership to buy your ships, and – the cherry on top – your very own personal player hab! Complete with a bedroom, a working desk, and even a bathroom, it’s a small slice of home in the vast Star Atlas universe. So, what’s coming in the next release? Expect the new star system, planets, and lore, the intro mission, the station liberation quest line, the inventory and crafting systems, ship buying, and all existing Galia features, plus basic hab placement. Further down the road, we’ll see career-specific quest lines and expanded hab utility, allowing you to build up your base and contribute to repairing the jump gate. Brad, Jose, and Santi, thank you for an absolutely epic ‘Brew’! Brad, you officially made everyone’s day 23% better, and we’re already calling for a part two! For those of you who joined, remember to claim your code “Brad” on SolquintBot – you have just a few minutes left! And don’t forget to join us this Friday for game night and the first Iris Blessing drawing. See you there, pioneers!
I am launching a Star Atlas merch store. Please check it out at Intergalactic Gear.
I am also creating a “non-guild” guild. If you are interested in learning more, please go to Intergalactic Coalition.
Star Atlas Weekly Recap Newsletter – Catch up on all the important videos and articles from the Star Atlas community in a weekly recap email newsletter. https://intergalacticherald.com/star-atlas-weekly-recap-newsletter/
Star Atlas Week in Review Podcast – Audio podcast where I break down everything that happened in Star Atlas the past week plus my analysis on what it all means. https://intergalacticherald.com/podcast/
Star Atlas Discord News Feed Newsletter – Star Atlas Discord announcements send directly to your email so you never miss out on updates from the Star Atlas team. https://intergalacticherald.com/star-atlas-discord-news-feed/
If you would like to support my efforts, donations can be sent to intergalacticherald.sol
